mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or out-of-sample predictive accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in housing demand, which transmits through financial leverage and wealth effects to produce negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 momentum as investors price in improved corporate cash flows.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day SPX returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, which consistently drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day SPX returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation crossing above 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: High inflation months fail to predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or the market prices in inflation without selling off (positive returns in > 60% of triggered cases).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between stagnant real GDP growth and falling real sales of existing homes signals a structural demand mismatch, leading to earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence metric shows no predictive power for 60-day SPX returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for credit spreads.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index crossing below zero signals immediate supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, prompting short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth fails to predict negative 5-day SPX returns, or the signal is overwhelmed by concurrent monetary easing announcements.
